---
title: "Development"
subtitle: "Contributing to the pipeline and documentation"
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
---

## Documentation Site

The `docs/` directory is a [Quarto](https://quarto.org/) website project. Pages
are authored as `.qmd` files and rendered to static HTML in `docs/_site/`.


### Rendering Locally

```bash
quarto render docs/
```

The output is written to `docs/_site/`. Open `docs/_site/index.html` in a
browser to preview. No pipeline output or R packages are required to render.

### Adding a New Page

1. Create a `.qmd` file in `docs/` with YAML front matter matching the existing
   pattern:

    ```yaml
    ---
    title: "Page Title"
    subtitle: "Short description"
    date: last-modified
    format:
      html:
        embed-resources: true
    ---
    ```

2. Add a navbar entry in `docs/_quarto.yml`:

    ```yaml
    - text: "Page Title"
      href: page-title.qmd
    ```

3. Add a bullet to the Reports list in `docs/index.qmd`.

4. Render and verify:

    ```bash
    quarto render docs/
    ```


### Site Configuration

Configuration lives in `docs/_quarto.yml`:

| Setting | Value | Purpose |
|---------|-------|---------|
| `project.type` | `website` | Multi-page site with shared navbar |
| `project.output-dir` | `_site` | Render target (git-ignored) |
| `website.title` | `nemascan-sims-nf` | Navbar brand text |
| `format.html.theme` | `cosmo` | Bootstrap theme |
| `format.html.toc` | `true` | Per-page table of contents |
| `format.html.toc-depth` | `3` | TOC heading depth |

Pages that set `embed-resources: true` produce self-contained HTML files with
all CSS/JS inlined, which is useful for sharing individual pages outside the
site context.


### Git-Ignored Paths

The rendered site and Quarto working files are git-ignored:

- `docs/_site/` &mdash; rendered HTML output (in `.gitignore`)
- `docs/.quarto/` &mdash; Quarto project cache (in `docs/.gitignore`)


## Publishing to GitHub Pages

The documentation site can be published as a GitHub Pages site for the
repository. There are two approaches depending on whether you want to commit the
rendered HTML or build it in CI.


### Option A: Publish from a Branch (No CI Required)

This approach uses the `quarto publish` command to push rendered HTML to a
dedicated `gh-pages` branch. No CI configuration is needed.

**One-time setup:**

1. Run `quarto publish` from the project root, targeting GitHub Pages:

    ```bash
    quarto publish gh-pages docs/
    ```

    This renders the site, creates or updates a `gh-pages` branch containing
    only the rendered output, and pushes it to the remote.

2. In the GitHub repository settings, go to **Settings > Pages** and configure:
    - **Source:** Deploy from a branch
    - **Branch:** `gh-pages`, folder `/ (root)`

The site will be available at
`https://andersenlab.github.io/nemascan-sims-nf/`.

**Branch note:** `quarto publish gh-pages` works from any branch. It renders
the `.qmd` files in your current working tree and pushes the output to the
`gh-pages` branch, regardless of which branch you are on. This means you can
preview-publish docs from a feature branch (e.g. `doc-updates`) before merging
to `main`.

**To update the published site** after changing `.qmd` files:

```bash
quarto publish gh-pages docs/
```

This is a manual step &mdash; the site is not automatically rebuilt on push.


### Option B: GitHub Actions (Automated Build on Push)

This approach uses a GitHub Actions workflow to render the site on every push to
`main` and deploy the output to GitHub Pages.

**One-time setup:**

1. In the GitHub repository settings, go to **Settings > Pages** and configure:
    - **Source:** GitHub Actions

2. Create `.github/workflows/publish-docs.yml`:

    ```yaml
    name: Publish Docs

    on:
      push:
        branches: [main]
        paths:
          - 'docs/**'
          - 'R/**'

    permissions:
      contents: read
      pages: write
      id-token: write

    concurrency:
      group: "pages"
      cancel-in-progress: false

    jobs:
      build:
        runs-on: ubuntu-latest
        steps:
          - uses: actions/checkout@v4

          - uses: quarto-dev/quarto-actions/setup@v2

          - name: Render site
            run: quarto render docs/

          - uses: actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3
            with:
              path: docs/_site

      deploy:
        needs: build
        runs-on: ubuntu-latest
        environment:
          name: github-pages
          url: ${{ steps.deployment.outputs.page_url }}
        steps:
          - id: deployment
            uses: actions/deploy-pages@v4
    ```

This workflow triggers only when files in `docs/` or `R/` change, keeping
unrelated pushes fast. The site is published to
`https://andersenlab.github.io/nemascan-sims-nf/`.


### Choosing an Approach

| Consideration | Option A (`quarto publish`) | Option B (GitHub Actions) |
|---------------|----------------------------|---------------------------|
| Setup effort | Minimal | Requires workflow file |
| Rebuild trigger | Manual command | Automatic on push to main |
| CI dependency | None | GitHub Actions |
| Best for | Infrequent doc updates | Docs that change with code |


## Running Tests

### Unit Tests

Unit tests use static fixtures and do not require pipeline output:

```bash
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

### Integration Tests

Integration tests require pipeline output collected by `tests/collect_test_data.sh`:

```bash
# Run pipeline and collect outputs
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh

# Or collect from a previous run
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --collect-only

# Run integration tests with collected data
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/.nf-work \
TEST_EXISTING_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

Integration test files skip automatically when the required environment
variables are not set.


## Pipeline Stub Runs

Stub runs verify process wiring without executing real computations:

```bash
# Fixed architecture
nextflow run main.nf -profile test -stub-run

# Variable architecture
nextflow run main.nf -profile test_variable -stub-run

# With legacy assessment path
nextflow run main.nf -profile test --legacy_assess -stub-run
```


## Generating Test Data

The test VCF is not committed to the repository due to its size. Regenerate it
from a source CaeNDR VCF:

```bash
./data/test/generate_test_vcf.sh /path/to/WI.20220216.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz
```

This produces `data/test/test.vcf.gz` (~825 MB, 13 strains, chromosomes I/II/V)
and its tabix index.


## Project Structure

```
nemascan-sims-nf/
 ├── main.nf                     Pipeline entrypoint
 ├── nextflow.config             Parameters and profiles
 ├── conf/                       Execution profiles (rockfish, docker)
 ├── modules/                    Nextflow process definitions
 │   ├── bcftools/               VCF manipulation processes
 │   ├── plink/                  LD pruning, binary fileset creation
 │   ├── gcta/                   GRM construction, GWAS mapping, simulation
 │   ├── python/                 Causal variant selection, variance check
 │   ├── r/                      Legacy QTL detection and assessment
 │   ├── db_migration/           DB-path ingestion, analysis, assessment
 │   └── local/                  Helper processes (contig info, eigens)
 ├── bin/                        Pipeline scripts (available to all processes)
 ├── R/                          Shared R library (sourced by db_migration + tests)
 ├── data/test/                  Test VCF, parameter files, strain lists
 ├── tests/                      R/testthat test suite
 └── docs/                       Quarto documentation site
```

Each module directory contains a `main.nf` with the process definition and
optionally a `resources/` directory with scripts added to `$PATH` via
`nextflow.enable.moduleBinaries = true`.
